California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Doris Christen 
City Clerk 
330 west Twentieth Avenue 
San Mateo, CA 
94403 
Dear Ms. Christen: 
October 6, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-542 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform 
Act1.· 
Your letter, dated September 18, 1989, correctly states our 
advice that because the general municipal election for the City of 
San Mateo was cancelled, the Act's provisions relating to pre-
election campaign statements would not apply. (Government Code 
• 
Section 84200.5.) 
A copy of your letter is enclosed for your 
convenience. 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional 
questions. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan

Enclosure 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK 
September 18, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Dear Sirs: 
FPPC 
SEP \9 \0 ~s ~M '89 
SilO WEST TWENTIETH AVENUE 
SAN MATEO. C AI..l FORNI A e .... 03 
TEI..EPHONE: C .. II11 377'3"20 
This is to inform you that the General Municipal Election scheduled 
to be held in the City of San Mateo on November 7, 1989 to elect 
three City Council Members was cancelled by the San Mateo City 
Council at their meeting of September 6, 1989. 
The election was 
cancelled in accordance with Elections Code Section 22843.5 and 
the three persons nominated were appointed to fill the three 
offices. 
Since there will be no election, it is my understanding after calling 
your agency that pre-election campaign statements will not be required 
to be filed. 
If this is not correct, please notify me right away 
so that September 28 filing deadlines can be met. 
Since all three 
nominees~were incumbents, they will be filing semi-annual statements 
covering this six-months period in January 1990. 
Sincerely, 
DORIS CHRISTEN 
CITY CLERK 


OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK 
September 18, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Dear Sirs: 
330 WEST TWENTIETH AVENUE 
SAN MATEO. CAL.IFORNIA 94403 
TEL.EPHONE: (415) 377-3420 
This is to inform you that the General Municipal Election scheduled 
to be held in the City of San Mateo on November 7, 1989 to elect 
three City Council Members was cancelled by the San Mateo City 
Council at their meeting of September 6, 1989. 
The election was 
cancelled in accordance with Elections Code Section 22843.5 and 
the three persons nominated were appointed to fill the three 
offices. 
Since there will be no election, it is my understanding after calling 
your agency that pre-election campaign statements will not be required 
to be filed. 
If this is not correct, please notify me right away 
so that September 28 filing deadlines can be met. 
Since all three 
nominees were incumbents, they will be filing semi-annual statements 
covering this six-months period in January 1990. 
Sincerely, 
OOR I S CHRI S TEN 
CITY CLERK 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Doris Christen 
City Clerk 
September 25, 1989 
330 West Twentieth Avenue 
San Mateo, CA 
94403 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-542 
Dear Ms. Christen: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on September 20, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely,

P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 •
